Author: Robert C. Martin
Key Insight: Names should reveal intent. A name that requires a comment to explain it has failed its job. Use pronounceable names so you can discuss code with colleagues without sounding strange.
Action Item: Rename all single-letter variables in current project except loop counters

Author: Robert C. Martin
Key Insight: Functions should do one thing and one thing only. If a function does more than one thing it should be split. The ideal function is 5-10 lines. Avoid flag arguments — they announce the function does two things.
Action Item: Audit existing functions over 20 lines and refactor

Author: Robert C. Martin
Key Insight: Don't return null — return empty collections or special case objects instead. Don't pass null either. Use exceptions rather than error codes. Write try-catch-finally blocks first.
Action Item: Replace all null returns in API handlers with typed error responses

Author: David Thomas / Andrew Hunt
Key Insight: Take responsibility for your code and your career. Don't live with broken windows — fix bad code when you see it or at minimum document it. Software entropy spreads fast once it starts.
Action Item: Identify 3 'broken windows' in current codebase and schedule fixes this sprint

Author: David Thomas / Andrew Hunt
Key Insight: DRY: every piece of knowledge must have a single authoritative representation. Duplication is not just copy-paste — it includes divergent code that represents the same concept in different ways.
Action Item: Audit codebase for logic duplicated across similar API routes

Author: Martin Kleppmann
Key Insight: Leader-based replication is simple but synchronous replication risks blocking writes if follower is down. Asynchronous replication is faster but risks data loss. Most systems use a hybrid approach with one sync follower.
Action Item: Implement basic read replica routing in side project DB config

Author: John Ousterhout
Key Insight: The best modules provide powerful functionality with a simple interface. A deep module has a small interface (low complexity cost) but rich implementation (high benefit). Avoid classitis — too many small shallow classes.
Action Item: Evaluate current service layer for opportunities to merge shallow wrapper classes
